header.title: Transparent pricing, no surprises
header.description: Each product has its own pricing. No hidden fees, no commitment.

items.question: Is there a minimum commitment?
items.answer: No. All subscriptions are commitment-free. You can cancel at any time.

items.question: Can I try before subscribing?
items.answer: Yes. YDV Insertion offers a free 1-month trial, no credit card required. Other products will offer the same at launch.

items.question: Can I use multiple products?
items.answer: Yes. Each product is independent with its own subscription. A single account gives you access to all your products.

items.question: Do prices include VAT?
items.answer: Prices shown are excl. VAT (EUR). French VAT (20%) applies for customers based in France. International clients may be subject to their local tax regulations.

items.question: What's the difference between monthly and annual billing?
items.answer: The annual plan saves you 20% compared to monthly billing. You're billed once a year, with no renewal commitment.

ia-suivi-beneficiaires-siae.title: How AI is transforming beneficiary tracking in social enterprises
ia-suivi-beneficiaires-siae.excerpt: Automatic summaries, pathway suggestions, dropout risk detection: a practical overview of AI use cases in work integration social enterprises.
content: Artificial intelligence is no longer reserved for big tech companies. In the work integration social enterprise (WISE) sector — organizations in France that employ people facing social and professional difficulties — AI is starting to concretely transform the work of employment counselors.
## Practical use cases
### 1. Automatic interview summaries

After each interview, the counselor writes a summary. This is a time-consuming task that can take 15 to 30 minutes. With AI, the counselor can dictate or note key points, and the AI generates a structured summary in seconds.
### 2. Personalized pathway suggestions

By analyzing the beneficiary's profile (skills, barriers, aspirations), AI can suggest suitable pathway options. The counselor retains final decision-making authority, but saves time on the analysis phase.
### 3. Early dropout risk detection
AI can identify weak signals: repeated absences, stagnation in progress, declining engagement. The counselor is alerted before the situation deteriorates.
### 4. Document generation
Reports, public service documents (ASP/ESF), correspondence: AI can generate drafts from existing data, which the counselor then validates and adjusts.
## The YDV Insertion approach
In YDV Insertion, AI is natively integrated with a multi-provider pattern (Claude, GPT-4, Gemini). The counselor chooses when and how to use AI. Nothing is automated without human validation.
## What AI doesn't replace
AI doesn't replace the human relationship. The trust between a counselor and a beneficiary cannot be automated. AI is a tool that frees up time to focus on what truly matters: personal support.

qualiopi-2026-changements.title: Qualiopi 2026: what's changing for training organizations in France
qualiopi-2026-changements.excerpt: New certification requirements, strengthened indicators, and how dedicated software can simplify compliance with France's mandatory Qualiopi quality label.
content: The Qualiopi framework — France's mandatory quality certification for training organizations — is evolving in 2026 with new requirements. These changes aim to strengthen training quality and transparency for funders.
## Key changes
### Strengthened indicators
Several framework indicators are being reinforced, particularly around outcome tracking and learner satisfaction. Organizations must now provide more detailed evidence of their quality approach.
### Digital attendance
Digital attendance tracking is becoming the standard. Paper-based attendance sheets are increasingly rejected during audits. A reliable electronic attendance system is now virtually essential.
### Post-training follow-up
Learner follow-up after training is reinforced. Organizations must demonstrate that they measure the medium-term impact of their training programs.
## How software can help
A dedicated management platform like YDV Formation allows you to centralize all documentary evidence, automate indicator collection, and prepare for audits with confidence.
Key features: structured course catalog, automatically generated agreements, digital attendance with QR codes, and real-time Qualiopi dashboards.
## Prepare now
Don't wait until the last moment to bring your processes into compliance. YDV Formation will be available soon — sign up to be notified at launch.

multi-provider-ia-architecture.title: Multi-provider AI: Claude, GPT-4 and Gemini in a single app
multi-provider-ia-architecture.excerpt: Architecture of a multi-provider AI routing system with automatic fallback. Lessons learned from CIP Platform.
content: When integrating AI into a business application, relying on a single provider is risky. Outages, pricing changes, rate limits: there are many reasons to want a multi-provider system.
## Why multi-provider?
### Resilience
If Claude goes down, the application automatically falls back to GPT-4. If GPT-4 is overloaded, Gemini takes over. The user notices nothing.
### Cost optimization
Different models are better suited to different tasks. Claude excels at synthesis and reasoning, GPT-4 performs well at code generation, Gemini offers excellent value for simple tasks.
### Independence
Not depending on a single provider is a sound software architecture practice.
## The chosen architecture
The system relies on a common `AIProvider` interface that each provider implements:
A central router handles provider selection based on the task, availability and user preferences.
## The fallback pattern
The fallback works in cascade: primary provider → secondary provider → tertiary provider. Each failure is logged, and the system learns to temporarily avoid unstable providers.
## Lessons learned

On CIP Platform (the platform behind YDV Systems), this pattern has been in production for over a year. It has absorbed several API outages with zero user impact, and optimized AI costs by 30% by routing simple tasks to less expensive models.
